title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Arthur Calgary interviews Mollie Craig, the former wife of Jack Agglesden, to discuss the circumstances surrounding Jack's arrest for the murder of his mother. Mollie describes Jack, whom she nicknamed "Monkey Face," as a charming manipulator. She recounts how he once used her money to bribe the wife of an auto repair shop owner to drop charges against him. As Calgary attempts to clear Jack's name, he finds himself increasingly disgusted by Jack's character. Mollie admits that while Jack claimed he did not intend to kill his mother, he did hit her with a poker during an attempt to get money to avoid prison. She believes Jack's aggressive nature and financial desperation led to the incident, expressing confusion when Calgary suggests Jack might be innocent.

Mollie explains that Jack kept their marriage secret to hide her from his upper-class family, claiming his mother would attempt to control her. She recalls a visit to the Agglesden home where a housekeeper refused to acknowledge her as Jack's wife. During that visit, a man named Mr. Aggles offered her a weekly allowance, which she continues to accept despite the disapproval of her current husband, Joe. Joe, who works in the power department, had previously warned Mollie against Jack. Mollie notes that Joe provided a sizable check as a wedding gift for her current marriage, hoping it would be more successful than her previous one.
